gaps: No test covers the behavior of Charge Stand name and image in Device Settings > Software tab when the Charge Stand is unplugged from power.
No test covers UI element replacement (name/image with placeholder) for a Charge Stand device based on power state (plugged vs unplugged).

No test covers the Device Settings > Software tab specifically for Charge Stand devices at all — existing tests focus on headset device images on the Home Screen, not Charge Stand-specific UI in sub-tabs.
No test covers power state transitions (unplugging from power) and their effect on device metadata display in the app.

generated_testcases.title: TC-CS-SW-001: Verify Charge Stand name and image display correctly when plugged into power
steps: 1. Ensure the Charge Stand is plugged into power via USB-C cable.
2. Open Poly Studio Mobile app on the connected phone.
3. Tap the Charge Stand device name on the Home Screen to enter Device Settings.
4. Tap the 'Software' tab (or scroll to the Software section).
5. Observe the device name displayed at the top of the Software tab.
6. Observe the device image/icon displayed next to or near the device name.
generated_testcases.expected: The Charge Stand's correct product name (e.g., 'Poly Charge Stand') is displayed. The correct Charge Stand product image/icon is displayed — not a generic placeholder image.

generated_testcases.title: TC-CS-SW-002: Verify Charge Stand name and image are replaced with placeholder when unplugged from power
steps: 1. Ensure the Charge Stand is plugged into power via USB-C cable.
2. Open Poly Studio Mobile app → tap the Charge Stand device → navigate to Device Settings > Software tab.
3. Verify the correct Charge Stand name and image are displayed (baseline).
4. Unplug the USB-C power cable from the Charge Stand.
5. Wait 10 seconds for the app to refresh the device state.
6. Observe the device name displayed at the top of the Software tab.
7. Observe the device image/icon displayed next to or near the device name.
generated_testcases.expected: After unplugging from power, the Charge Stand's product name is replaced with a generic placeholder name (e.g., 'Device' or 'Charge Stand' without model details). The product image is replaced with a generic placeholder icon (e.g., a gray silhouette or default device icon).

generated_testcases.title: TC-CS-SW-003: Verify Charge Stand name and image restore when re-plugged into power
steps: 1. Start with the Charge Stand unplugged from power.
2. Open Poly Studio Mobile app → tap the Charge Stand device → navigate to Device Settings > Software tab.
3. Verify the placeholder name and image are displayed (baseline for unplugged state).
4. Plug the USB-C power cable back into the Charge Stand.
5. Wait 10 seconds for the app to refresh the device state.
6. Observe the device name at the top of the Software tab.
7. Observe the device image/icon.
generated_testcases.expected: After re-plugging into power, the Charge Stand's correct product name and image are restored — the placeholder is replaced with the proper device metadata.

generated_testcases.title: TC-CS-SW-004: Verify Charge Stand placeholder persists after app restart while unplugged
steps: 1. Ensure the Charge Stand is unplugged from power.
2. Open Poly Studio Mobile app → tap the Charge Stand device → navigate to Device Settings > Software tab.
3. Verify the placeholder name and image are displayed.
4. Press the Home button to background the app.
5. Fully close (force-kill) the Poly Studio Mobile app from the app switcher.
6. Reopen Poly Studio Mobile app.
7. Tap the Charge Stand device → navigate to Device Settings > Software tab.
8. Observe the device name and image.
generated_testcases.expected: While the Charge Stand remains unplugged from power, the placeholder name and image persist across app restart. The correct product name and image are NOT restored until the device is plugged back into power.

generated_testcases.title: TC-CS-SW-005: Verify Charge Stand placeholder behavior during power state transitions (plug → unplug → plug)
steps: 1. Ensure the Charge Stand is plugged into power.
2. Open Poly Studio Mobile app → tap the Charge Stand device → navigate to Device Settings > Software tab.
3. Verify the correct Charge Stand name and image are displayed.
4. Unplug the Charge Stand from power.
5. Wait 5 seconds and observe the Software tab UI — do not navigate away.
6. Re-plug the Charge Stand into power.
7. Wait 5 seconds and observe the Software tab UI — do not navigate away.
8. Unplug the Charge Stand from power again.
9. Wait 5 seconds and observe the Software tab UI.
generated_testcases.expected: Step 5: The name and image change to placeholder within a few seconds of unplugging. Step 7: The correct name and image are restored within a few seconds of re-plugging. Step 9: The placeholder reappears after unplugging again. The UI correctly reflects the power state on each transition without requiring navigation away and back.

generated_testcases.title: TC-CS-SW-006: Verify other tabs are NOT affected by Charge Stand power state changes
steps: 1. Ensure the Charge Stand is plugged into power.
2. Open Poly Studio Mobile app → tap the Charge Stand device → navigate to Device Settings.
3. Navigate to a non-Software tab (e.g., 'Audio' or 'General' settings tab).
4. Verify the device name displayed in the header/title bar shows the correct Charge Stand name.
5. Unplug the Charge Stand from power.
6. Wait 10 seconds.
7. Observe the device name in the header/title bar of the current (non-Software) tab.
8. Navigate to the Software tab and observe the device name and image.
generated_testcases.expected: Step 7: The device name in the header/title bar of non-Software tabs remains correct (not replaced with placeholder). Step 8: The Software tab shows the placeholder name and image. The placeholder replacement is isolated to the Software tab's device metadata display and does not affect other tabs or the navigation header.

generated_testcases.title: TC-CS-SW-007: Verify Charge Stand placeholder appears on Software tab when navigating from another tab after unplugging
steps: 1. Ensure the Charge Stand is plugged into power.
2. Open Poly Studio Mobile app → tap the Charge Stand device → navigate to Device Settings.
3. Navigate to a non-Software tab (e.g., 'Audio' tab).
4. Unplug the Charge Stand from power.
5. Wait 10 seconds.
6. Tap the 'Software' tab to navigate to it.
7. Observe the device name and image displayed on the Software tab.
generated_testcases.expected: When navigating to the Software tab after the Charge Stand has been unplugged, the placeholder name and image are displayed (not the cached correct name/image from when the device was plugged in).

generated_testcases.title: TC-CS-SW-008: Verify Charge Stand placeholder behavior during Bluetooth disconnect while unplugged
steps: 1. Ensure the Charge Stand is unplugged from power.
2. Open Poly Studio Mobile app → tap the Charge Stand device → navigate to Device Settings > Software tab.
3. Verify the placeholder name and image are displayed.
4. Disconnect Bluetooth from the Charge Stand (turn off Bluetooth on phone or disconnect from device).
5. Wait 5 seconds.
6. Reconnect Bluetooth to the Charge Stand.
7. Wait 10 seconds for the app to reconnect.
8. Tap the Charge Stand device → navigate to Device Settings > Software tab.
9. Observe the device name and image.
generated_testcases.expected: Step 4-5: The app may show 'Disconnected' state. Step 9: After reconnecting while the Charge Stand remains unplugged, the placeholder name and image are still displayed. The placeholder state is tied to power state, not Bluetooth connection state.
